<result>Confirms these are near-identical copies of the same upstream `nrwl/nx-ai-agents-config` skills, just slightly older/differently formatted, further confirming they were copied out of that repo into `.agents/skills/` rather than being made available to Claude Code as an actual plugin.

## Summary

**What the "workspace skill/plugin" is meant to be:** Nx ships an official Claude Code plugin (`nx@nx-claude-plugins`) sourced from `github:nrwl/nx-ai-agents-config`. It bundles the `nx-workspace`, `nx-generate`, `nx-import`, `nx-run-tasks`, `nx-plugins`, `monitor-ci`, and `link-workspace-packages` skills plus an MCP server. `/home/skogix/workspace/CLAUDE.md` (auto-generated by Nx, between the `<!-- nx configuration start/end -->` markers) explicitly tells the agent to "invoke the `nx-workspace` skill" and "ALWAYS invoke the `nx-generate` skill FIRST" for scaffolding.

**Root cause — the plugin was declared but never actually installed:**

- `/home/skogix/workspace/.claude/settings.json` correctly *declares* the marketplace and plugin:
  ```json
  "extraKnownMarketplaces": { "nx-claude-plugins": { "source": { "source": "github", "repo": "nrwl/nx-ai-agents-config" } } },
  "enabledPlugins": { "nx@nx-claude-plugins": true }
  ```
- But Claude Code's **global** plugin registries at `/home/skogix/.claude/plugins/known_marketplaces.json` and `/home/skogix/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json` have **no entry at all** for `nx-claude-plugins` or `nx@nx-claude-plugins`. Compare to the marketplaces that *are* registered there (worktrunk, openai-codex, claude-plugins-official, skogai-market, skoghooks) — each has a corresponding install under `~/.claude/plugins/cache/<marketplace>/...`. Nothing exists under `~/.claude/plugins/cache/nx-claude-plugins/` or `~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/nx-claude-plugins/`.
- Because the marketplace was never actually added/trusted and the plugin never installed, Claude Code has no `nx-workspace`/`nx-generate` skill registered anywhere it scans, even though `settings.json` says it should be enabled. This is exactly the "plugin declared in settings.json but marketplace/plugin add was never run/approved" failure mode — likely because `enabledPlugins` alone doesn't trigger auto-fetch/trust of a brand-new marketplace without an explicit `/plugin marketplace add nrwl/nx-ai-agents-config` (or manual confirmation) having happened.
- Meanwhile, the actual SKILL.md files for `nx-workspace`/`nx-generate` (and the others) **do exist in the repo**, but only under `.agents/skills/` and `.opencode/skills/` (committed in `9d0266a "chore(nx): add workspace tooling and skills"`). These directories are for other agent tools (Codex/OpenCode style "agents" convention) — **Claude Code does not scan `.agents/` or `.opencode/` for skills**, only `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (project-level) or an actually-installed plugin's skill directory. There is no `.claude/skills/` directory in this repo at all (`find .claude -maxdepth 4` shows only `settings.json` and `data/sessions/`).
- Corroborating evidence: `/tmp/nx-ai-agents-config/9609810013/` is a full checkout of the upstream `nrwl/nx-ai-agents-config` repo (numeric suffix looks like a CI run id), containing `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` and `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` — the proper plugin manifests — plus a `generated/` folder that fans the same skill content out per-tool (`.agents`, `.codex`, `.opencode`, `.gemini`, `.cursor`). This strongly suggests someone ran that repo's own generation/sync script and copied its `generated/.agents` and `generated/.opencode` output into this workspace, instead of installing it as a Claude Code plugin via `/plugin marketplace add` + `/plugin install`.

**Not the cause:** The Nx workspace/project-graph configuration itself is healthy — `nx.json` has valid `plugins` entries for `@nx/vite/plugin` and `@nx/docker`, and `bunx nx show projects` runs cleanly returning `["@workspace/api-e2e","@workspace/api","@workspace/source"]` with no errors. There's no local custom Nx plugin (no `generators.json`/`executors.json` anywhere outside `node_modules`), so this isn't an Nx plugin-discovery problem — it's purely a Claude Code plugin/skill-installation gap.

**Fix (for the user to action, not performed here since this is read-only):** Either (a) actually install the plugin — `/plugin marketplace add nrwl/nx-ai-agents-config` then `/plugin install nx@nx-claude-plugins` (or equivalent trust/approval flow) so it lands in `~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/` and `~/.claude/plugins/cache/`, or (b) if plugin installation isn't desired, copy the skill content from `.agents/skills/nx-workspace` and `.agents/skills/nx-generate` into a proper `.claude/skills/nx-workspace/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/nx-generate/SKILL.md` in this repo so Claude Code's project-level skill loader picks them up directly.</result>
